title: Storage, Filesystems, Archives, and Backups · Operations Case: Block Filesystem Mount
summary: Respond to low disk space and protect service data without confusing filesystems, mounts, archives, snapshots, and verified backups. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux block filesystem mount increment in a storage map and restore-tested backup plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.
Plan configuration preservation, cache use, downgrade constraints, service impact, and verification for package rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Block Filesystem Mount
steps.instruction:
theory case — Filesystem / is 92% full with 200 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Block Filesystem Mount contract for the supplied data.
